plan_adherence_notes: All 17 planned steps were completed. Steps 1-3 (file setup, fixtures, beforeEach/afterEach) match exactly. Steps 4-16 (13 individual tests) were all implemented faithfully — step 8 was improved by splitting into two tests (plural and singular) instead of one. Step 17 (run vitest) confirmed all 14 tests pass. The only minor deviation is the test count (14 vs 13 originally planned) due to the beneficial split of step 8. No planned tests were skipped or materially changed.
missed_questions: Should maxLength enforcement on the textarea be tested (e.g., attempting to type beyond 2000 chars)?

Should the test verify that trimmedBody is passed (not raw body) — i.e., a whitespace-padded input results in a trimmed payload? (This was implicitly tested in step 13 but not explicitly asked as a question.)
How should the component behave if comments.data is present but pagination is missing or malformed?
reason: Execution closely followed the plan with all 17 steps completed. The plan was well-grounded in thorough research that correctly identified the mocking pattern (TradingPanel/EventTradePanel style), fake timers for deterministic timestamps, crypto.randomUUID availability, and the onSuccess callback invocation pattern. All 14 tests pass and cover loading, error, empty, populated, singular/plural count, unconfigured session, composer form, read-only, disabled submit, submit+clear, pending mutation, mutation error, and character count states. The test file follows established codebase conventions. Missed questions are minor edge cases that don't affect the core test coverage.
